title: Book Of Nonsense Limerick 47.
content:
There was a Young Lady of Poole,
    Whose soup was excessively cool;
    So she put it to boil,
    By the aid of some oil,
    That ingenious Young Lady of Poole.
meaning:
"Book Of Nonsense Limerick 47." is a quintessential example of Edward Lear's signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
